
 Bottom line

 Not safe as currently deployed: the control token (= shell RCE) sits in
 plaintext in a world-readable log and the public view token is weak. The code
 itself has no injection vulnerabilities and a reasonable auth model — fixing
 secret hygiene (logging, file perms, token strength) and adding headers +
 throttling closes the meaningful gaps.
╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 Claude has written up a plan and is ready to execute. Would you like to proceed?

 ❯ 1. Yes, auto-accept edits
   2. Yes, manually approve edits
   3. Tell Claude what to change
      shift+tab to approve with this feedback
